Welcome to FinTree

You already sensed that — it's why you're here, and not looking for a shortcut. For fourteen years we've had one job: making finance finally click, so understanding arrives before the exam does.

Careers launched

Three respected paths, honestly compared.

Before you commit years to a credential, see them side by side — on cost, time-to-earn, and global mobility, one keeps pulling ahead.

Our pick for finance careers

CFA

Chartered Financial Analyst

Best forThe global gold standard for investment roles — equity research, portfolio mgmt, wealth, risk
The path3 exams, fully self-paced (~300 hrs/level) — study alongside college or a job
Typical duration~2–4 years, at your pace, not a fixed calendar
Approx cost (IN)~₹4–5.5 lakh all-in — a fraction of an MBA, with no lost salary
Typical IN payL1 ~₹4–6 LPA; charterholder ~₹20–40 LPA in core finance; ₹50 L–₹1 cr+ in senior investment roles, globally

CA

Chartered Accountancy

Best forAccounting, audit & tax practice — strong in India, narrower globally
The path3 levels + a mandatory 2-year articleship on a modest stipend
Typical duration~4.5–5 years on a fixed calendar
Approx cost (IN)~₹1–4 lakh in fees — but two articleship years at low pay
Typical IN payFresher ~₹7–13 LPA; in finance roles the ceiling typically sits below CFA peers

MBA (Finance)

Management degree

Best forGeneral management & leadership — finance depth varies widely by college
The pathA year of CAT/XAT prep, then a 2-year full-time degree, off the job market
Typical duration2 years full-time, plus entrance prep
Approx cost (IN)~₹5–30 lakh — up to 6× the CFA — plus two years of foregone salary
Typical IN payOnly top-20 B-schools reach ₹25–36 LPA; the wider market trails well behind — at 6× the entry cost

scroll to compare →

Figures are approximate for 2026 and vary by registration timing, college, and attempts. For a career in core finance, the CFA comes out ahead on almost every count — that is why we teach it.

But before we map where finance can take you, it helps to know who's walking beside you.

Who's walking beside you

You'd be in good company.

The mind behind the method.

The person behind the method

I think you have to be a good learner before you're any good as a teacher. I borrow from Feynman — if I can't break a concept down to first principles and make it simple, I don't understand it well enough yet. That's the standard every FinTree class is held to.

— Utkarsh Jain, Founder

Chartered Accountant · CFA · FRM · CFP · LLB. Advisor to family offices and UHNIs. Visiting faculty at India's top B-schools.

Why capable people still fail CFA.

It's rarely intelligence. It's three quiet gaps — concepts memorised but never understood, practice that started too late, and an exam strategy no one actually taught. Name them, and they stop being fatal.

Concepts

Memorised, but never understood.

Practice

Started too late to hold under pressure.

Strategy

An exam plan no one actually taught.

If those are the three cracks people fall through, here's how we close each one.

The method

How FinTree actually helps: the LPR method.

Three gaps. Three deliberate answers — a sequence, not a slogan.

L

Learn. concept clarity first

You understand why before you ever memorise what.

P

Practice. pressure, rehearsed

Cases, drills, past-paper rigor, weekly and mock tests — so exam pressure meets prepared hands.

R

Revise. until it holds

A revision system, and mentors, that stay with you until it holds.

Know the terrain before you climb it.

CFA Level I is ten subjects, from Quantitative Methods to Ethics, and about 10–12 months of focused study. Eligible with a bachelor's degree — or within 23 months of graduating, so B.Com and BBA students can sit Level I in their second year. No degree? 4,000 hours of relevant work experience counts too.

Exam structure

FormatComputer-based · 2 sessions
Duration2 × 2 hr 15 min
Questions180 MCQs · 90 per session
Exam windowsFeb · May · Aug · Nov

Three answer choices per question, no negative marking, about 90 seconds a question. One Practical Skills Module (10–20 hrs, ungraded) completes your result.

The ten subjects · exam weightage

Quantitative Methods 6–9% Economics 6–9% Financial Statement Analysis 11–14% Corporate Issuers 6–9% Equity Investments 11–14% Fixed Income 11–14% Derivatives 5–8% Alternative Investments 7–10% Portfolio Management 8–12% Ethical & Professional Standards 15–20%

CFA Level II is the same ten subjects at valuation depth — application over recall, tested through case-style item sets. Another 10–12 months of honest study, with a passed Level I in hand.

Exam structure

FormatItem sets — vignette + 4 MCQs
Duration2 × 2 hr 12 min
Questions22 item sets · 88 MCQs
Exam windowsMay · Aug · Nov

Each vignette is a mini case study — answers come from the case, not from memory. Twenty sets are scored, two are unscored trials. No negative marking; a new Practical Skills Module applies here too.

The ten subjects · exam weightage

Quantitative Methods 5–10% Economics 5–10% Financial Statement Analysis 10–15% Corporate Issuers 5–10% Equity Investments 10–15% Fixed Income 10–15% Derivatives 5–10% Alternative Investments 5–10% Portfolio Management 10–15% Ethical & Professional Standards 10–15%

CFA Level III is where it all becomes portfolio management: a common core on strategy and reporting, plus one specialised pathway you pick. You write constructed answers here — the exam reads your thinking, not just your options.

Exam structure

FormatEssays + item sets
Duration2 × 2 hr 12 min
Questions11 essay sets + 11 item sets
Exam windowsFeb · Aug

The core is 65–70% of the paper; your chosen pathway is the other 30–35%. Written answers are graded by charterholders — clear, direct reasoning scores.

The core · 65–70% of the exam

Asset Allocation 15–20% Portfolio Construction 15–20% Performance Measurement 5–10% Derivatives & Risk Management 10–15% Ethical & Professional Standards 10–15%

Choose one pathway · 30–35%

Portfolio Management Private Wealth Private Markets

A method and a mode are promises. Here's the proof we keep them.

Where this leads

Where this leads.

More than 130 firms hire from FinTree — from Morgan Stanley, JP Morgan, and HSBC to Crisil, Motilal Oswal, and ICICI Prudential. Roles land in about five days on average, across a ₹1.2L–26L range, and roughly a quarter of our hiring partners come back for more. We don't place you; we prepare you — with skills, projects, and interview training — until the offer is earned.

₹1.2L–26L
p.a.

~20
Roles shared monthly

And when you're ready to take the next small step, you won't be filling out a form.

When you're ready

When you're ready, talk to a person.

No checkout, no pressure — just a real conversation with someone who's guided this exact decision hundreds of times. Ask them anything, even whether this is the right path for you at all.
